metadata.scene_name: Mass Distribution in a Rotating Disc
metadata.task: Apply the same torque test to the rotating disc setup and determine which mass distribution would produce the larger angular acceleration. Return 1 for mass concentrated near the center, 2 for mass concentrated near the rim, and 3 for no meaningful difference.
metadata.description: Use the disc and support as the rotation setup, and compare angular response under the same applied torque. Base the answer on observed rotational response or a measured inertia proxy, not on the label alone.

expected_behavior: The LLM compares rotational response and identifies the lower-inertia distribution as producing greater angular acceleration.
reasoning: Concentrating mass near the center lowers rotational inertia for the same torque, so the numeric distribution code is 1.
